fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area; Sidewalk Network
fr.considerations: Multiple Access Points; Fenced Sections; Limited Rear Access
fr.recommendations: Enhancing the clarity and visibility of the multiple access points on the property by using distinctive markings or signage could assist emergency responders in quickly identifying entry and exit routes, potentially improving response times. Ensuring these access points are unobstructed and wide enough for emergency vehicles may enhance their maneuverability. The presence of fenced sections suggests evaluating emergency access and egress adaptability, such as the integration of gates that could be easily unlocked or removed in case of an emergency. Additionally, considering modifications to facilitate rear access could support comprehensive coverage of the property during response activities. Maintaining a clear and well-defined sidewalk network may aid responder movement and navigation, ensuring they can efficiently reach all key areas of the property when required.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Trees; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain; Open Fuel Break; Shrubs Under Trees; Developed Fuel Break
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space and reducing fuel continuity around the property can be achieved by focusing on several key areas. Managing vegetation immediately adjacent to the structure by trimming or clearing could help minimize potential exposure. Maintaining separation between scattered trees and removing shrubs growing under them can further disrupt fuel continuity and reduce opportunities for fire spread. Preserving and possibly expanding existing open terrain and fuel breaks between the vegetated perimeter and the structure can support effective suppression operations and contribute to long-term property resilience. Continued attention to managing vegetation within developed fuel breaks and keeping them clear of new growth will support their effectiveness. Regularly assessing and maintaining these areas will ensure they remain functional in promoting defensible space and enabling firefighter access.
